title: gh#41: Phase 11 follow-up: SnippetPicker arrow-nav + Enter regression test
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/41.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Context

Phase 11 UAT (test 8) confirmed the snippet picker keyboard navigation works via the cmdk-backed primitives in `packages/web/src/components/ui/command.tsx` — arrow-key navigation, Enter selection, and Escape are inherited from cmdk. The existing Vitest tests cover Escape close + focus-return, substring filtering, cursor-position insertion, and selected-range replacement.

What's missing is an explicit guard that **arrow-key + Enter** still inserts the highlighted snippet at the cursor. cmdk covers it today, but a guarded regression test is cheap insurance against a future cmdk swap or upgrade silently breaking the contract.

## Acceptance criteria

- New Vitest case in `packages/web/src/components/snippets/__tests__/SnippetPicker.test.tsx`:
  1. Render the picker with multiple snippets
  2. Press `ArrowDown` to move highlight off the first item
  3. Press `Enter`
  4. Assert the **highlighted** snippet's body was inserted at the captured cursor position (not the first item, not nothing)
- All existing tests in the file still pass.

## Why now

Discovered as a non-blocking gap during Phase 11 UAT (see `.planning/phases/11-snippets-search-calendar-polish/11-UAT.md` test 8 note). Filed for later because Phase 11's substance ships green without it.

recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#41 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
